title: ‘Shots on goal and win the game’: NASA’s effort to accelerate lunar landings
summary: NASA is increasing the frequency of robotic lunar lander missions to support its lunar exploration goals. However, the agency has not yet detailed plans to speed up the development of crewed lunar landers.

title: Starfish Space finds a new partner for docking demonstration mission
summary: Starfish Space has secured a new partner for its docking demonstration mission after its original collaborator withdrew. The company is now proceeding with an alternative partner for the mission.

title: Artemis 2 astronauts arrive at KSC
summary: The four Artemis 2 astronauts arrived in Florida on March 27 for final preparations ahead of a launch scheduled as soon as April 1. This marks a key milestone in NASA's Artemis program.

title: Commercial Space Federation (CSF) Welcomes Two New Associate Members
summary: The Commercial Space Federation (CSF) announced the addition of two new associate members, Astrolab and Zeno Power, which focus on planetary mobility and energy solutions for extreme environments.

title: ESA to decide by June on Europe’s Gateway contributions
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will decide by June on its contributions to NASA's Gateway lunar-orbiting station, following NASA's recent decision to halt work on the project. This decision comes as Europe re-evaluates its role in U.S. lunar ambitions amid shifting Artemis program priorities.

title: India’s Bellatrix raises $20 million following overseas expansion drive
summary: India-based Bellatrix Aerospace raised $20 million to expand production of satellite propulsion systems after securing its first major overseas commercial customer. The funding follows the company's push to grow internationally.

title: Building a hypersonic pathway to orbit with Mark Bigham
summary: The Space Minds podcast features a discussion with Mark Bigham, Vice President of Defense Programs at Longshot Space Technologies Corporation, about developing a hypersonic pathway to orbit. The episode highlights the company's new hardware startup efforts in this area.

title: The ‘ground truth’ gap in AgTech: Why satellites alone can’t save supply chains
summary: Satellite technology has revolutionized precision agriculture and supply chain monitoring, but 'ground truth' verification remains a challenge. AgTech relies on more than just satellites to ensure accuracy in data.

title: Industry says proposed NASA changes to commercial space station plans create confusion
summary: NASA's proposed changes to its support for commercial space stations have caused concern and confusion among industry companies. The head of an industry organization has warned about the potential impact of these changes.

title: SBQuantum and Spire to send quantum diamond magnetometer into orbit
summary: Canadian startup SBQuantum will send a quantum diamond magnetometer into low-Earth orbit on March 30 aboard a Spire Global satellite launched by SpaceX's Falcon 9. Spire will provide the satellite, ground stations, and data processing for the mission.

title: Kristen Stewart will play Sally Ride, the 1st American woman in space, as Amazon finally greenlights mini-series 'The Challenger'
summary: Kristen Stewart will portray Sally Ride, the first American woman in space, in Amazon's newly greenlit mini-series 'The Challenger'. This marks Stewart's TV series debut.

title: NASA's Artemis 2 moon astronauts are 'fortunate' to have a private space toilet — Apollo crews pooped in plastic bags
summary: NASA's Artemis 2 astronauts will have a private space toilet, unlike Apollo crews who used plastic bags. The new toilet system marks a significant upgrade in astronaut comfort and hygiene.

title: 'We work for them, too': Artemis 2 moon mission is for everyone, NASA astronaut Victor Glover says (video)
summary: NASA astronaut Victor Glover emphasizes the importance of engaging diverse communities with the Artemis 2 moon mission, not just achieving milestones. He highlights the mission's broader impact beyond technical achievements.

title: 'It's all I think about': Artemis 2 commander Reid Wiseman is zeroed in on historic moon mission
summary: NASA astronaut Reid Wiseman, commander of Artemis 2, emphasizes his focus on the historic moon mission, which will be the first in over 50 years. He expresses determination to ensure the mission's success.

title: Making Canadian history: Artemis 2 astronaut Jeremy Hansen is ready for his epic moon mission
summary: Jeremy Hansen will become the first Canadian astronaut to fly around the moon as part of NASA's Artemis 2 mission. Growing up in a country without astronauts, he is making history in space exploration.

title: 'We're all on this journey together:' Who are the Artemis 2 astronauts launching to the moon?
summary: NASA announced the four astronauts selected for the Artemis 2 mission, which will be the first crewed lunar flyby in over 50 years. The crew includes astronauts from the U.S. and Canada, representing international collaboration.

title: 'An incredible privilege and responsibility': Artemis 2's Christina Koch is ready to become the 1st woman to fly around the moon
summary: Christina Koch is preparing to become the first woman to fly around the moon as part of NASA's Artemis 2 mission. She expressed the significance of this opportunity as both a privilege and responsibility.

title: Where should we send a real 'Hail Mary' spacecraft? A new study has the answers
summary: A new study proposes sending a spacecraft to one of 45 potentially habitable planets, inspired by the book 'Project Hail Mary' by Andy Weir. The research includes an atlas of these planets for potential future missions.

title: Astrophotographer captures spectacular photo of Antennae Galaxies dueling in deep space
summary: An astrophotographer captured a stunning image of the Antennae Galaxies merging into a single elliptical galaxy. This cosmic event showcases the dynamic process of galactic collision and evolution.

title: This Week In Space podcast: Episode 203 — China Rising
summary: This Week In Space podcast episode 203 discusses China's rising influence in space and potential threats to American leadership, featuring Dr. Namrata Goswami from Johns Hopkins University.

title: I Am Artemis: Erik Richards
summary: Erik Richards, Near Space Network Mission Manager, discusses his role in supporting NASA's first crewed Artemis mission to the Moon and back. His career has focused on enabling spacecraft communication with Earth.

title: NASA Selects Intuitive Machines to Deliver Artemis Science, Tech to Moon
summary: NASA awarded Intuitive Machines a $180.4 million contract to deliver science and technology payloads to the Moon under the CLPS initiative and Artemis program. The mission includes seven payloads, five of which are NASA-funded, to advance lunar exploration and research.

title: NASA’s Environment and Energy “Blue Marble” Awards Categories
summary: NASA has announced the categories for its Environment and Energy 'Blue Marble' Awards, recognizing excellence in environmental management and project execution. The awards highlight achievements in compliance, conservation, and stakeholder partnerships.

title: NISAR’s View of Mount Rainier
summary: NISAR, a U.S.-Indian Earth satellite, captured an image of Mount Rainier on November 10, 2025, using its L-band SAR instrument to penetrate cloud cover. The satellite's data provides detailed surface observations of the Pacific Northwest region.

title: NISAR Views Mount St. Helens
summary: The U.S.-Indian Earth satellite NISAR captured an image of Mount St. Helens on November 10, 2025, using its L-band SAR instrument to penetrate clouds and reveal the surface below.

title: NASA Tech and Science Bound for Low Earth Orbit on Commercial Launch
summary: NASA's technology and science demonstrations will launch to low Earth orbit aboard a SpaceX Falcon 9 rocket as part of the Transporter-16 rideshare mission. The demonstrations aim to test thermal protection systems, advance communications, and deepen understanding of Earth's atmosphere.

title: NASA Names Scientists to Support Lunar South Pole Science
summary: NASA has selected 10 scientists to assist in planning lunar surface science activities for the Artemis program, including instrument deployment and sample collection. The team will help shape mission objectives for astronauts exploring the Moon's south pole.

title: I Am Artemis: Michael Guzman
summary: Michael Guzman, main propulsion systems engineer for NASA's Artemis II mission, discusses his passion for rocket science as reflected in his email signature. His work focuses on key propulsion metrics like thrust and specific impulse.

title: NextSTEP-3 E: Network Extension for User Continuity and Sustainability (NEXUS) Ka-Band Backward-Compatible Relay Broad
summary: NASA plans to release a BAA under NextSTEP-3, Appendix E, for Project NEXUS to acquire a Ka-band backward-compatible relay service as the TDRS system declines. The project aims to provide end-to-end relay services including space, ground, launch, integration, and operations.

title: Artemis II Crew’s Suits
summary: NASA and CSA unveil the Orion Crew Survival System suits for the Artemis II crew, including Commander Reid Wiseman, Pilot Victor Glover, Mission Specialist Christina Koch, and Mission Specialist Jeremy Hansen, ahead of their test flight mission.

title: SpaceX to deliver 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit on Transporter 16
summary: SpaceX is preparing to launch 119 payloads to Sun-synchronous orbit aboard the Transporter 16 mission from California. This marks another significant rideshare mission for the company.

title: NASA outlines Moon Base plans, pivots on Gateway
summary: NASA announced detailed plans for a Moon base during its 'Ignition' event, shifting focus away from the Lunar Gateway. This marks a significant pivot in the agency's lunar exploration strategy.

title: Isar Aerospace scrubs second launch of Spectrum rocket
summary: Isar Aerospace, a European private space launch provider, has scrubbed the second launch attempt of its Spectrum rocket. The company, based in Ottobrunn, Germany, had planned the launch but faced an unexpected delay.

title: NASA unveils Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars in 2028
summary: NASA announced the Space Reactor-1 Freedom mission to Mars, planned for launch in 2028. The initiative was unveiled by Administrator Jared Isaacman and other agency leaders.

title: Progress on Starbase Pads ahead of Block 3 Starships
summary: SpaceX is accelerating groundwork at Starbase in preparation for upcoming Block 3 Starship launches, highlighting efforts to increase launch cadence. Engineers are actively working on infrastructure upgrades to support the next generation of Starship vehicles.

title: Launch Preview: Russia to debut Soyuz-5; Falcon 9 and Atlas V to launch internet satellites
summary: Russia is set to debut its new Soyuz-5 rocket, while the U.S. will launch Falcon 9 and Atlas V rockets carrying internet satellites. Multiple countries, including China, New Zealand, and Norway, are also preparing for upcoming launches.

title: Progress MS-33 launches to ISS from repaired pad
summary: Progress MS-33 successfully launched to the International Space Station (ISS) from the repaired Baikonur Cosmodrome pad. The mission follows repairs made to the launch site after a previous launch.

title: Blue Origin ramps up New Glenn manufacturing, unveils Orbital Data Center ambitions
summary: Blue Origin is increasing the production rate of its New Glenn heavy-lift rocket and has announced plans to develop an Orbital Data Center. The company aims to expand its space infrastructure capabilities.

title: SLS enters pad flow ahead of historic Artemis II mission
summary: NASA's Space Launch System (SLS) rocket and Orion spacecraft have arrived at the launch pad in preparation for the historic Artemis II mission. This marks a significant milestone ahead of the planned lunar flyby mission.

title: Booster 19 concludes initial test campaign on Pad 2
summary: Booster 19 has completed its initial test campaign on Pad 2, marking another milestone for SpaceX's Starship Flight 12. This brings the next launch one step closer to realization.

title: TEC Business to Business website
summary: The TEC Business to Business website reported on a space industry-related topic, but no specific details were provided in the given news snippet.

title: ESA Delivers: 50 years booklet
summary: ESA celebrates its 50th anniversary with a booklet highlighting 50 major achievements over the past five decades. The publication underscores the agency's milestones in space exploration and technology.

title: Proba-3’s Coronagraph is alive!
summary: The Proba-3 mission's Coronagraph spacecraft has re-established contact with ground control after a month-long anomaly caused a loss of communication.

title: Upcoming ESA–Industry events on CM25 implementation
summary: ESA is hosting events to discuss the implementation of CM25, the Council Meeting at Ministerial Level held in November 2025. These events will highlight planned activities, thematic priorities, and industrial opportunities within ESA programs.

title: Plato readies for space-like tests
summary: ESA's Plato mission, designed to hunt for Earth-like exoplanets, is undergoing critical space-like tests in the Large Space Simulator chamber. The spacecraft, equipped with 26 ultrasensitive cameras, is scheduled for launch on an Ariane 6 rocket in January 2027.

title: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visits ESTEC
summary: Former NASA astronaut Jeanette Epps visited ESTEC, the European Space Agency's technical center. This visit highlights international collaboration in space exploration.

title: ESA rolls out new costing software for its suppliers
summary: The European Space Agency (ESA) will introduce ECOS version 6 on March 30, 2026, making it mandatory for all suppliers to use this new costing software in their financial proposals for ESA tender actions.

title: The tiny lens antenna with big potential
summary: A PhD researcher developed a 3D-printed, football-sized lens antenna to enhance satellite navigation resilience against interference, offering a simpler and cheaper alternative to bulky existing solutions.

title: Work ongoing to restore contact with Proba-3's Coronagraph
summary: An anomaly onboard the Proba-3 mission’s Coronagraph spacecraft caused a loss of contact with ground control. Teams are investigating the issue and working to restore communication.

title: Students kick off experiments on ESA’s flat floor
summary: Students have initiated experiments on ESA's flat floor facility, which simulates low-gravity environments for research. This marks a collaborative effort to advance space science and technology.

title: Xona Space Systems Raises $170M Series C to Scale Private GPS
summary: Xona Space Systems has raised $170 million in a Series C funding round to expand its private GPS technology, which offers stronger, more accurate, and secure PNT signals in low Earth orbit compared to traditional GPS.

title: Jared Isaacman Hits 100 Days In Office With a Bang
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman marked 100 days in office with an ambitious announcement, emphasizing a return to the Moon. The statement suggests a renewed focus on lunar missions under his leadership.

title: With Artemis Changes, Europe is Left Holding the Bag
summary: ESA and European space primes invested heavily in components for the lunar orbiting station, but recent Artemis program changes have left them with unfulfilled commitments. Europe now faces financial and strategic challenges due to these adjustments.

title: Arkadia Space Secures €14.5M to Develop New Biprop Engine
summary: Arkadia Space, a Spanish propulsion startup, secured €14.5 million from the European Investment Council to develop a new bipropellant engine. This funding marks a significant step in advancing their propulsion technology.

title: Lawmakers Question NASA’s Shifting Vision for CLDs
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman's new plan for human presence in Low Earth Orbit (LEO) has raised concerns among lawmakers, who question the shifting vision for Commercial Low Earth Orbit Destinations (CLDs). The proposal has left many legislators confused about the agency's direction.

title: Directed Energy: An Alternative to Space-Based Interceptors
summary: Directed energy is presented as an alternative to traditional space-based interceptors (SBIs) in a recent article. The post explores this technology as a potential solution for space defense.

title: Commerce Unveils One-Stop Licensing Proposal For Novel Space Missions
summary: The U.S. Department of Commerce proposed a one-stop licensing system for novel space missions, aiming to clarify regulatory authority over missions that don't fit existing frameworks. This initiative is part of an ongoing effort to streamline mission authorization amid jurisdictional ambiguities.

title: PAVE Space Raises $40M for In-Space Propulsion Tech
summary: PAVE Space secures $40 million in funding to advance in-space propulsion technology using storable bipropellant, aiming to simplify and reduce the cost of launch operations compared to cryogenic fuels.

title: Airbase Emerges From Stealth With $5M to Automate Spectrum Allocation
summary: Airbase, a new startup, has emerged from stealth mode with $5 million in funding to automate spectrum allocation for the growing number of space missions. This initiative aims to address the increasing demand for spectrum management as orbital activities expand.

title: NASA Priorities Get a Major Makeover
summary: NASA Administrator Jared Isaacman has introduced a new plan to shift NASA's priorities towards a commercially driven lunar economy with surface infrastructure. This marks a significant change in NASA's strategic direction.

title: Bridgit Mendler Outlines Northwood’s Vision for Rapidly Deployable Ground Infrastructure
summary: Bridgit Mendler discussed Northwood Space's efforts to address satellite ground infrastructure challenges by creating a rapidly deployable end-to-end ground system. The initiative aims to streamline satellite operations and improve efficiency in the space industry.

title: Interview With AvL CTO Chris Rose at SATShow Week
summary: AvL Technologies CTO Chris Rose discusses the evolution of tactical fly-away systems during an interview at SATShow Week. The discussion highlights advancements in this technology from AvL's booth.

title: D2D, Sovereign Demand, and Mega-Valuations: Space Leaders Navigate Market Volatility
summary: Space industry leaders at SATShow expressed uncertainty about the market's outlook amid volatility. Discussions included the impact of direct-to-direct (D2D) services and sovereign demand on valuations.

title: Interview With Qorvo Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings at SATShow Week
summary: Qorvo's Director of Satcom & Systems Engineering Ryan Jennings discusses the company's RF solutions at SATShow Week, highlighting their applications in LEO satellite phased arrays.

title: Sovereign Satcom Networks Grapple With Data Security Amid Geopolitical Uncertainty
summary: Nations are increasingly prioritizing sovereign satellite communication networks to enhance data security amid rising geopolitical tensions. The challenge lies in defining the scope and capabilities of sovereign space infrastructure in a complex global environment.

title: Investors Debate IPO Path, Financing Routes Needed to Grow Space Market
summary: Investors discuss the need for improved financing routes, including IPOs, to support the growing commercial space sector. Venture capitalists highlight that financing has not kept pace with the industry's technological advancements.

title: Interview With Kepler Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis at SATShow Week
summary: Kepler Communications' Chief Revenue Officer Beau Jarvis discusses the company's strategy for downlinking data from space to Earth during SATShow Week. The interview highlights Kepler's approach to addressing this key challenge in the space industry.

title: The Evolving Influence of IFC Driving Network Development
summary: Airlines have embraced inflight connectivity (IFC) as a standard offering for passengers over the past two decades. IFC developers are now focusing on expanding and enhancing these networks to meet growing demand.

title: Government Investment, Regulations Are the Key Trends to Watch in India, APAC Space
summary: Government investment and regulatory frameworks are identified as critical trends driving the growth of the space economy in India and the Asia-Pacific region. The role of governments as investors and market creators is expected to dominate the sector's development.

title: Interview With Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski at SATShow Week
summary: Comtech Satellite & Space President Daniel Gizinski discusses the first delivery of Comtech’s Digital Common Ground 7000 series modems at SATShow Week, featuring live multi-orbit demonstrations.
